patch.value:
## Context
Fourth ecosystem/backlog audit (follows pm-w7f2, pm-y904, pm-o043). Goal: confirm the backlog fully reflects the entire pm-cli ecosystem — commands, core subsystems, SDK, MCP, extensions/packages, tests, docs — organized per best-practice PM, with zero duplicates. Working tree already carries audit #3's charter + active-item metadata backfill (uncommitted).

## Method
- Codebase inventory via Explore agent (7 categories: CLI commands, core subsystems, SDK, MCP, extension platform, tests, docs).
- Cross-referenced every subsystem against the 11 living capability epics + milestone pm-doxj.
- pm stats / health / validate for hygiene; pm search per subsystem for coverage; parent-linkage + dep graph inspection.

## Findings & actions (recorded as notes/learnings on this item)
1. Structural gap: no open Governance/validation capability epic → created pm-33cw; re-parented pm-j8z6/e9zh/pivf (were on milestone directly).
2. Coverage: broad — every other subsystem maps to an existing epic (matrix in notes).
3. Hygiene: hierarchy clean, all active items fully specified, all 5 validate warnings map to open items.

## Definition of done
Coverage matrix recorded; governance epic + re-parenting done; concurrency dep graph modeled; milestone body enumerates capability epics; pm validate re-run with no new warnings; findings captured in notes/learnings. No commit/push (per instruction).
2026-07-04 ecosystem audit #4: coverage matrix, governance capability epic & relationship modeling

Every codebase subsystem maps to exactly one capability epic (coverage matrix recorded); no orphan open items; every pm validate/health warning maps to an open item; no duplicate items created; relationship graph modeled for the concurrency cluster.
Verify the pm backlog fully and non-redundantly reflects the entire pm-cli ecosystem per best-practice PM

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
COVERAGE MATRIX (audit #4, 2026-07-04) — every codebase subsystem maps to exactly one living capability epic under milestone pm-doxj. Built from Explore codebase inventory (28 CLI commands, ~20 core subsystems, SDK, 28 MCP tools, 11 first-party packages, 263 specs, 14 docs).

CLI command surface (lifecycle/query/bulk/scheduling/logs/links/help, plan family, focus/session) -> pm-mpbb
Storage + item model + io/fs/output + checkpoint rollback GC -> pm-o2kc
History (append/replay) + compact/redact/repair + drift-scan -> pm-o2kc
Search (bm25/vector/hybrid) + eval harness + background-refresh -> pm-ydp6
Extensions runtime + loader + 11 packages + SDK (define/compose/runtime/testing) + plugins (pm-claude/pm-codex) -> pm-ugqx
Governance + validate + diagnostics/remediation registry + metadata-coverage/issue-codes/content-fields -> pm-33cw (NEW this audit)
Concurrency: lock ownership/gc, claim/release atomicity, singleton-file races -> pm-dj98
Telemetry + observability + sentry instrumentation -> pm-5oj5
Config (nested-settings) + schema (types/statuses/fields/workflows) + profiles (archetype presets) -> pm-8jdc
MCP server + machine-readable contracts (cli-contracts) -> pm-5k4v
Source-code quality gates: CodeFactor/ESLint/jscpd/LOC/CC ratchets -> pm-92if
Docs + onboarding + release + CI + build harness (scripts/ *.mjs) -> pm-u9d0
Architecture Decision Records (living decision log, 50 Decisions) -> pm-m2u1
Post-v0.1 roadmap container / milestone -> pm-doxj (Milestone)

RESULT: 12 living capability epics + ADR log + milestone cover 100% of shipped subsystems. No subsystem is orphaned; no two epics overlap (scope In/Out boundaries stated in each charter). No duplicate epics created — pm-33cw continues closed governance epics pm-5rjn/pm-wtsp rather than duplicating them.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Governance/validation was the single uncovered capability: it had been folded into pm-mpbb (command surface) and the closed epics pm-5rjn/pm-wtsp, leaving validate-remediation chores (pm-j8z6/e9zh/pivf) orphaned directly on milestone pm-doxj. Created pm-33cw. Durable rule: source-code quality (pm-92if: CodeFactor/ESLint/LOC/CC) and data/tracker governance (pm-33cw: validate/health/metadata-coverage/remediation) are DISTINCT capabilities and must never be merged.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: VERIFICATION COMPLETE. (1) In-flight uncommitted code (lock.ts +80, claim.ts +17, constants/errors/types) maps to in_progress pm-2muu (lock retry) + pm-8t5x (claim atomicity) — no untracked code. (2) Zero TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers in src/ — no untracked work hidden in comments. (3) PRD post-v0.1 roadmap items (cross-encoder rerank pm-7tsx, embedding batch pm-i25f, vector adapters pm-kj4) are all CLOSED/implemented — no untracked forward work. (4) pm next surfaces sensible actionable work; pm aggregate confirms clean epic distribution. (5) All 5 pm validate warnings map to open items under pm-33cw. No duplicate items created. Only 2 new items: pm-33cw (governance epic, fills real gap) + pm-osea (this tracker).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
AGENT-STORY LAYER (audit #4). Created 12 capability-level agent-stories, one per capability epic, tag=agent-story+capability+living-map+story: pm-nh73(mpbb), pm-hu11(o2kc), pm-nnro(ydp6), pm-m2kl(ugqx), pm-tra4(33cw), pm-miju(dj98), pm-gnya(5oj5), pm-0zuv(8jdc), pm-wo7x(5k4v), pm-r0z2(92if), pm-ixm6(u9d0), pm-xugp(m2u1).

CONVENTION: capability-stories describe the enduring agent-value each shipped capability serves ("As a <role>, I want <capability>, so that <context-management outcome>"). Status=closed with resolution/expected/actual because the capabilities are delivered (matches the all-stories-closed house convention); Story type is excluded from the generated CHANGELOG so no drag; parented to the epic so the value statement lives alongside the living backlog. "Living set" = the tier is maintained and grows with the epics; add a new capability-story when a genuinely new capability ships.

TWO-TIER, NON-DUPLICATIVE: these capability-stories are umbrella statements distinct from the pre-existing FEATURE-level stories already parented under the same epics (e.g. pm-mpbb also has pm-9mjw sprint-closeout, pm-kp1d context projection, pm-ankr changed-since; pm-ydp6 also has pm-arew search-scope, pm-fhsg eval-harness). Tier is queryable via `pm list --type Story --tag agent-story --status all`.

CORRECTION: `pm list --json` compact projection OMITS parent AND tags fields; use `pm get <id>` to read them. Earlier "stories unparented" reading was that projection quirk — the 24 historical stories were already epic-parented.

patch.value: Audit #4 complete. Verified the backlog fully + non-redundantly reflects the pm-cli ecosystem (12-epic coverage matrix recorded). Structural fixes: created governance epic pm-33cw + re-parented pm-j8z6/e9zh/pivf; modeled pm-khdq<->pm-bgcu concurrency edge; milestone pm-doxj now indexes all 12 epics. Per user direction, added a 12-story capability agent-story layer (one per epic, tag=agent-story+capability, closed=delivered) complementing the existing feature-level stories. 14 new items total; only open/new items touched (zero changelog drag); no duplicates; not committed.
The pm backlog fully reflects the entire ecosystem per best-practice PM with an agent-story layer and no duplicates.
12 living capability epics cover 100% of subsystems; governance gap closed; agent-story layer added; all 5 validate warnings map to open items; pm health ok:true.

Audit #4 complete. Verified the backlog fully + non-redundantly reflects the pm-cli ecosystem (12-epic coverage matrix recorded). Structural fixes: created governance epic pm-33cw + re-parented pm-j8z6/e9zh/pivf; modeled pm-khdq<->pm-bgcu concurrency edge; milestone pm-doxj now indexes all 12 epics. Per user direction, added a 12-story capability agent-story layer (one per epic, tag=agent-story+capability, closed=delivered) complementing the existing feature-level stories. 14 new items total; only open/new items touched (zero changelog drag); no duplicates; not committed.
